free_text: Yields of radionuclides formed in the interaction
of 3.65 A*GeV 12C-ions and protons with nat-Pb
The target stacks for this experiment consisted of
three foils from natural lead. The outer foils were
used for recoil loss compensation, the middle one was
The target foils, varying in thickness from 45 to
120 um/cm2 and surrounded by 175 um/cm2 Mylar catcher
Irradiation periods were chosen to achieve
The measurements of the radioactivity induced
in the target foils began one hour after irradiations
The spectra were fitted the Gaussian peaks.
The errors assigned to the yields are based
A systematic error of about 10% corresponding
to the beam flux monitor is not included
